ddls:
CREATE TABLE "assessment_sets" (
  "set_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each assessment set */,
  "set_name" TEXT /* Name of the assessment set */,
  "set_type" TEXT /* Type of the assessment set (e.g., written, spoken) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the assessment set was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the assessment set */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the assessment set (e.g., created, in progress, completed) */,
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instructor who created the assessment set */,
  "class_id" INTEGER /* ID of the class the assessment set belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assessment_sets_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("class_id") REFERENCES classes ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_assessment_sets_instructor_id FOREIGN KEY ("instructor_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_types" (
  "error_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error type */,
  "error_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the error type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the error type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the error type (e.g., grammar, vocabulary, punctuation) */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the error (e.g., 1 for minor, 5 for major) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_counts" (
  "error_count_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error count entry */,
  "set_id" TEXT /* Reference to the assessment set */,
  "error_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the error type */,
  "error_count" INTEGER /* Count of the error type in the assessment set */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student response */,
  "context" TEXT /* Context for the error (e.g., sentence or paragraph where the error occurred) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_count_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_counts_error_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_type_id") REFERENCES error_types ("error_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_counts_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("set_id") REFERENCES assessment_sets ("set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_counts_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES student_responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., teacher, researcher, admin) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the role (JSON format) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the role was created */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date the role was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the assessment set */,
  "set_id" TEXT /* ID of the assessment set being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the assessment set was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser and device) used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("set_id") REFERENCES assessment_sets ("set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "set_id" TEXT /* ID of the assessment set the report is generated for */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., error frequency, summary) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report file */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("set_id") REFERENCES assessment_sets ("set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student response */,
  "set_id" TEXT /* ID of the assessment set the response belongs to */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student who provided the response */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of the response (e.g., written, spoken) */,
  "response_text" TEXT /* Text or transcript of the response */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date the response was submitted */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade or score given to the response */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Feedback on the response */,
  "is_flagged" INTEGER /* Indicates if the response is flagged for review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_responses_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("set_id") REFERENCES assessment_sets ("set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_responses_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Full name of the student */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "class_id" INTEGER /* ID of the class the student belongs to */,
  "dob" TEXT /* Date of birth of the student */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the student */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the student record was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("class_id") REFERENCES classes ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the class */,
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the teacher responsible for the class */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the class */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the class */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the class is conducted (e.g., online, classroom) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the class */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the class is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_classes_teacher_id FOREIGN KEY ("teacher_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
